P(A).description: Probability that the product is good (not defective), derived from the 5% defect rate.

P(A_c).description: Probability that the product is defective (5% failure rate from the assembly line).

P(B|A).description: Probability that the test indicates good given the product is actually good.

P(B_c|A).description: Probability that the test indicates defective given the product is actually good (complement of P(B|A) = 1 - 0.96).

P(B_c|A_c).description: Probability that the test indicates defective given the product is actually defective.
